mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2014 payroll growth of 288k persons indicates strong labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce higher average 20-day returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.5% annually, signaling accommodative monetary policy that sustains low equity discount rates and supports forward equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2014 FEDFUNDS at 0.1% and CPI at 2.1% YoY
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Mechanism fails if triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or if Spearman rank correlation between the interaction signal and forward returns is < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 900,000 annualized units, indicating strong residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through multiplier effects on materials, labor, and financial services.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 HOUST at 1,001,000 units, highest since 2008
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in post-crisis expansion
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if HOUST > 900k triggers produce positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or if Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates or turns negative month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and forward economic slack, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 INDPRO at 103.66, showing recent volatility
failure_condition: Mechanism fails if negative INDPRO months produce negative 5-day returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or if Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating synchronized consumer and residential demand strength that amplifies forward earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through joint demand multiplier effects.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2014 RSAFS at 439,891M and HOUST at 1,001k indicate synchronized demand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in early-to-mid cycle recovery
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in < 58% of instances, or if the interaction signal's Rank IC is < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5% in a single month, signaling deterioration in labor market conditions and forward earnings pressure, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2014 UNRATE at 6.1%, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Mechanism fails if UNRATE > 6.5% produces negative 20-day returns in < 54% of triggered instances, or if Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating a widening real rate gap that signals monetary accommodation relative to growth, supporting positive equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2014 GDPC1 growth at 1.8%, FEDFUNDS at 0.1%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy supporting above-trend growth
failure_condition: Hypothesis rejected if the interaction signal produces positive 60-day returns in < 60% of triggered instances, or if Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized supply-side and labor market weakness that compresses forward earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50000
evidence_refs: fact:May 2014 INDPRO and June 2014 PAYEMS show continued growth, below trigger
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction indicating recessionary pressure
failure_condition: Mechanism fails if joint trigger produces negative 20-day returns in < 56% of instances, or if the interaction signal's Rank IC is < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
